# Foxconn Industrial Internet (FII) — NVL72 ODM Assembler
Last updated: 2026-04-01

## Parent Company
- **Hon Hai Precision Industry Co., Ltd.** (Foxconn) — Taipei, Taiwan
- **FII (Foxconn Industrial Internet)** — Shanghai Stock Exchange listed subsidiary (601138.SS)
- The data center / AI factory division operates under FII brand
- Revenue: Hon Hai group ~$200B+ USD annually
- Chairman: Young Liu (Hon Hai)

## Role in NVL72 Supply Chain
- **Primary ODM assembler** for NVIDIA NVL72 compute tray assemblies
- Foxconn manufactures the server tray chassis, integrates GPU modules, and assembles the internal liquid cooling loop (tray inner manifold, coldplate connections, QD fittings)
- One of NVIDIA's highest-volume ODM partners for Blackwell
- Also manufactures complete NVL72 rack assemblies for hyperscale customers

## Products / Components in NVL72 Context
- Tray chassis fabrication (sheet metal, structural)
- Internal manifold routing within compute tray
- Cold plate integration (Boyd/Eaton cold plates installed at Foxconn)
- Staubli UQD fitting installation (per-tray connections)
- Full tray assembly, test, and burn-in

## Data Center Division
- FII Smart Manufacturing: AI factory design + manufacturing
- FII Cloud Infrastructure: server/rack ODM for hyperscalers
- US manufacturing presence: expanding in Wisconsin (existing) and Texas
- Customer base: Microsoft, Apple, Amazon (ODM/EMS), NVIDIA

## ADC Relevance
- Understanding Foxconn's role = understanding where NVL72 tray manifold bottlenecks originate
- Lead time for assembled trays (including manifold loop) flows through Foxconn
- Not a direct ADC vendor — this is supply chain intelligence

- FII Data Center Business Group — contact via fii.com.cn
- Young Liu (Hon Hai Chairman) — public figure, LinkedIn presence
